content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges (numbered 111 through MMM). GGG does not contain any self-loops, but it may contain parallel edges. For each valid iii, the iii-th vertex has a non-negative integer weight AiA_iAi​. Also, for each valid iii, the iii-th edge has a non-negative integer weight BiB_iBi​.

Let E={1,2,…,M}E = \{1, 2, \ldots, M\}E={1,2,…,M} denote the set of edges and for any subset S⊆ES \subseteq ES⊆E, let V(S)V(S)V(S) denote the set of vertices which are endpoints of at least one edge from SSS. You should find
max⁡S⊆E,∣S∣≤K(∑v∈V(S)Av−∑e∈SBe) .\max\limits_{S\subseteq E, |S| \le K} \left(\sum\limits_{v \in V(S)} A_v - \sum\limits_{e \in S} B_e\right) \,.S⊆E,∣S∣≤Kmax​(v∈V(S)∑​Av​−e∈S∑​Be​).

In other words, among all subsets of at most KKK (but possibly zero) edges, you should find the largest value of the expression "sum of weights of their endpoints, where each endpoint is counted only once, minus the sum of weights of these edges". Note that SSS may contain multiple parallel edges with the same weights ― they are still considered distinct.
constraints: 1≤ui,vi≤N1 \le u_i, v_i \le N1≤ui​,vi​≤N for each valid iii
ui≠viu_i \neq v_iui​=vi​ for each valid iii
GGG does not contain any cycles or parallel edges
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​ and BiB_iBi​, describing an edge between vertices uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ with weight BiB_iBi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the largest value of the given expression.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

The largest value is obtained when SSS contains only edges (2,5)(2, 5)(2,5) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4). In that case, V(S)V(S)V(S) contains the vertices 2,3,4,52, 3, 4, 52,3,4,5.
